programmes.show_description: The celebrity team come to the end of their fast-track training to dive with 18-foot tiger sharks and relocate to the world-famous dive site of Tiger Beach - after getting one last chance to hone their skills and demonstrate that they are ready to take part, the qualified team members come face to face with the iconic shark. Will the celebrities have their perceptions changed in a life-changing encounter, are they still monsters to be scared of, or is their biggest fear an ocean without sharks?
News, sport and weather from across Scotland

Jason Williams visits a community allotment in Liverpool and Martha Swales shares a seed sowing masterclass. Camilla Bassett-Smith explores a pristine Yorkshire garden and Henry Agg provides a step-by-step guide to building a raised bed. Alan shares his expert advice on growing potatoes and managing ferns
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day one of the prestigious festival, where the feature race is the 4.00 Unibet Champion Hurdle, and races also take place at 1.20, 2.00, 2.40, 3.20 and 4.40. Analysis comes from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh, Mick Fitzgerald and Megan Nicholls, with reports by Matt Chapman, Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, Social Stable updates from Oli Bell, betting news with Brian Gleeson, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

Bradley Walsh hosts as Steven from Stirling, Phia from Amersham, Jack from Birmingham and Helen from Loughborough work as a team to take on one of the experts in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

programmes.show_name: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programmes.show_description: The cash expert is back with all the latest need-to-know financial headlines of the week in his topical Big Briefing. He also takes viewers' questions live and in News You Can Use, he draws attention to all the current deals people won't want to miss
Cain needs to face up to what his future might hold, while Joe feels unappreciated and Kim is surprised when she pays a visit to Moira
Gary intervenes to reconcile Todd and Theo, Maggie orders Lauren to show Ollie what he's missing, Alya receives a concerning message, and Leanne overhears Megan flirting on the phone

Just six climbers remain on the journey to reach the top of one of New Zealand's highest peaks, but there is no let-up in the punishing terrain and terrifying crossings. As the mountain claims more victims and the Mountain's Keeper delivers another brutal message, Dockers seizes an opportunity for more tactical manoeuvring before the climbers have to scale a vast ice wall
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories

Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of Paula Beer, who is searching the son she gave up for adoption 57 years ago, and Sharon Thomas, who is looking for the younger brother she didn't know existed until she was in her twenties

The broadcaster travels to Northern Ireland to explore the culinary scene in Belfast, a city blessed with a bounty of food and drink. Dermot also meets chef and restaurateur Gemma Austin and finds out about Fifteens, an iconic snack in the city
News, sport and weather from across Scotland

Jeremy is joined by TV presenter Chris Bavin, starting in the city's outskirts by diving into the world of regenerative farming, before doing a spot of line fishing. The real catch of the day, however, might be a tour of Hong Kong's last traditional roasting pits for Chris
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Coverage of day two of the prestigious festival, where the feature race is the 4.00 BetMGM Queen Mother Champion Chase, and races also take place at 1.20, 2.00, 2.40, 3.20 and 4.40. Analysis comes from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh, Mick Fitzgerald and Megan Nicholls, with reports by Matt Chapman, Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, Social Stable updates from Oli Bell, betting news with Brian Gleeson, and commentary from Richard Hoiles
Four players answer trivia questions. Then as a team, face a quiz champion (The Chaser) to win thousands of pounds in a timed final quiz. Bradley Walsh hosts
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Good Morning Britain's Kate Garraway and This Morning's Ashley James battle singers Toyah Willcox and Carol Decker to win big money for charity
Rhona expresses her gratitude, but is spotted by Lydia, while Laurel confides in her friend Nicola
Todd's friends rally round to support him, Adam and Jodie's feuding turns dirty, Leanne consults Daniel about Sam, and Ollie chastises Maggie for meddling in his affairs

A flashback shows Rebecca preparing to abduct Lucia in southern France as she reveals shocking news to Elisa's daughter in the present day. Meanwhile, Selma continues to investigate Elisa and discovers her dark past. Thriller, starring Holliday Granger and Denise Gough
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories

Political magazine show hosted by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme
